name: Laguna XS 2.1
archDisplay: 33B-parameter sparse Mixture-of-Experts (3B active/token), 256 experts + 1 shared (top-8), 40 layers (10 global + 30 sliding-window attention, 3:1), grouped-query attention with FP8 KV cache, 256K-token context, native interleaved reasoning (toggleable per request)
note: Poolside's compact open-weight coding model, released 2026-07-02 -- a 33B-parameter (3B active) sparse MoE for agentic software engineering, and the smaller sibling that Laguna S 2.1 scales up from (same pre-training data). An upgrade of Laguna XS.2 with a +5.4-point jump on SWE-bench Multilingual (63.1% vs 57.7%) and a license move from Apache-2.0 to OpenMDW-1.1. 256K-token context, text-only, native interleaved reasoning (toggleable per request). Ships BF16/FP8/INT4/NVFP4 checkpoints plus a DFlash 0.5B speculator; compact enough to run on a Mac with ~36 GB unified memory (BF16 download ~67 GB). Served first-party via platform.poolside.ai and on OpenRouter/Ollama/NVIDIA NIM at $0.10/M input, $0.20/M output ($0.05/M cache-read), 256K context. All benchmarks are Poolside self-reported (coding-only suite); no independent re-evaluation exists yet.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, official Poolside HF model card (directly re-verified: 70.9% for XS 2.1 vs 69.9% for the predecessor XS.2). Poolside separately reports SWE-bench Multilingual 63.1% (+5.4pt vs XS.2's 57.7%), recorded only in prose here since the swe-bench slug tracks the Verified variant.
SWE-Bench Pro (Public Dataset), official HF model card (directly re-verified: 47.6% vs 46.3% for XS.2).

Terminal-Bench 2.0, official HF model card (directly re-verified: 37.5% vs 35.7% for XS.2). Filed under terminal-bench-2 with the version noted, matching this corpus's precedent of recording both TB 2.0 and TB 2.1 scores under this slug (cf. Claude Opus 4.1/4.5 TB 2.0 rows). The later Laguna S 2.1 uses the newer TB 2.1 harness.

verificationNotes:
ROUND (2026-07-22): Added alongside the Poolside org and Laguna S 2.1. Laguna XS 2.1 predates Kimi K3 (released 2026-07-02) but was never ingested (Poolside was entirely absent from the corpus); included to give Poolside complete coverage and because S 2.1 is explicitly a scale-up of it. All three recorded benchmarks were re-verified by direct fetch of the official HF model card (huggingface.co/poolside/Laguna-XS-2.1): SWE-bench Verified 70.9%, SWE-bench Multilingual 63.1%, SWE-Bench Pro 47.6%, Terminal-Bench 2.0 37.5% -- confirmed verbatim, with the predecessor XS.2's values (69.9/57.7/46.3/35.7) also shown. The launch blog's benchmark charts render as '0.0' in fetched HTML (JS-hydrated), so the HF card is the citation of record; the blog independently confirms the Multilingual 63.1% figure, params (33B/3B), 256K context, OpenMDW-1.1 license, 2026-07-02 date, and $0.10/$0.20/$0.05 pricing in plain text.

RANKING: 3 tracked benchmarks all in the coding category -> below the D20 gate (needs >=2 categories), so UNRANKED, same as Laguna S 2.1 and correct for a coding-only model. Discarded a secondary source (aimadetools.com) claiming 'Terminal-Bench 2.1 = 33.4%' -- a different benchmark version that cannot be reconciled with the primary HF card's TB 2.0 37.5%; primary source used. Predecessor is null because Laguna XS.2 is not in the corpus. Self-reported-only caveat as for Laguna S 2.1.
